proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with feature_035=0.

analyses.result_summary: Among 3,624 feature_035=1 patients, response rate is 22.13% vs 16.50% in 46,376 feature_035=0 patients. Univariate logistic regression gives log-OR = 0.364 (OR = 1.44).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_011 are associated with a lower rate of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 is 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders. Per 1-SD increase, log-OR = -0.155 (OR = 0.856).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_006 (range 20-100, plausibly age) are associated with a lower rate of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_006 is 58.2 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders. Per 1-SD increase, log-OR = -0.109 (OR = 0.897).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate differs across the three levels of feature_057 (0, 1, 2).
Higher levels of the ordinal feature_057 monotonically decrease the odds of objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_057)', data=df).fit() vs intercept-only LRT
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: feature_057=0 21.29% (n=17,592), =1 15.52% (n=24,971), =2 11.19% (n=7,437). LR test against null gives chi-square on 2 df.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_057', data=df).fit()  # treating as numeric
analyses.result_summary: Treating feature_057 as numeric, log-OR per level = -0.384 (OR = 0.681 per increment); the monotonic decrease holds.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_099 are associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
Higher values of feature_063 are associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher values of feature_092 are associated with a lower rate of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_099 is 3.82 in responders vs 3.80 in non-responders. Per 1-SD, log-OR = +0.058 (OR = 1.059).
Mean feature_063 is 5.65 in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders. Per 1-SD, log-OR = -0.064 (OR = 0.938).
Mean feature_092 is 19.07 in responders vs 20.40 in non-responders. Per 1-SD, log-OR = -0.056 (OR = 0.946).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate differs across racial categories in feature_005 (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Objective_response rate differs across insurance categories in feature_087 (medicare, medicaid, private, uninsured).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_005)', data=df).fit() vs null LRT
analyses.result_summary: Rates: white 17.28%, hispanic 16.70%, asian 16.33%, black 15.74%, other 14.72%. LR chi-square on 4 df.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_087)', data=df).fit() vs null LRT
analyses.result_summary: Rates: medicare 17.18%, medicaid 16.87%, private 16.66%, uninsured 16.62%. No detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035, feature_011, feature_006, feature_057, feature_099, feature_063, and feature_092 each remain independently associated with objective_response after mutual adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_099 + feature_063 + feature_092 + feature_084 + feature_035 + feature_093 + feature_121 + feature_014 + feature_012 + C(feature_057) + C(feature_005)', data=df_std).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the multivariable model: feature_035 OR=1.43 (p<1e-16); feature_057=2 vs 0 OR=0.46 (p<1e-16); feature_011 OR/SD=0.856 (p<1e-16); feature_006 OR/SD=0.896 (p<1e-16); feature_099 OR/SD=1.061 (p=9.6e-7); feature_063 OR/SD=0.936 (p=1.3e-6); feature_092 OR/SD=0.943 (p=9.6e-6). All retain significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035=1 on objective_response is modified by feature_057 severity level (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction LR test on 2 df: p=0.92. The effect of feature_035 does not differ across feature_057 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_035 on objective_response is modified by feature_006 (the relative benefit may differ for older vs younger patients).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term coef = +0.036 (per 1-SD age in feature_035=1). Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_011 on objective_response is attenuated in patients with feature_035=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term coef = -0.054 per 1-SD; p=0.225. No detectable interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_099 on objective_response is attenuated (or reversed) in patients with feature_035=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term coef = -0.094 per 1-SD; in feature_035=0 patients, feature_099 is positively associated, but this association is reduced in feature_035=1 patients. Statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each level of feature_057 (0, 1, 2), feature_035=1 increases objective_response rate compared to feature_035=0.

analyses.code: stratified logistic regression of feature_035 by feature_057 level
analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0: 27.0% vs 20.8%, OR=1.40, p=1.5e-7. feature_057=1: 20.5% vs 15.1%, OR=1.44, p=2.4e-9. feature_057=2: 15.3% vs 10.9%, OR=1.48, p=2.2e-3. Direction consistent across all strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate decreases monotonically across quartiles of feature_006 (Q1 lowest values to Q4 highest values).

analyses.result_summary: Q1 (20.0-46.6): 18.96%. Q2 (46.7-60.0): 17.48%. Q3 (60.1-73.3): 16.50%. Q4 (73.4-100.0): 14.67%. Monotonic decline across quartiles. Q4 vs Q1 absolute diff -4.3 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate decreases monotonically with increasing feature_011 quartiles.

analyses.result_summary: Bins (after dropping duplicate edges due to zero-floor): low (0-3.0) 18.57%, mid (3.1-6.4) 16.82%, high (6.5-23.6) 13.59%. Monotonic decline; absolute drop ~5 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_006 on objective_response is modified by feature_057 severity level.

analyses.result_summary: LR test on 2 df: p=0.49. No interaction; feature_006 acts additively on the log-odds across feature_057 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 and feature_011 interact in their effect on objective_response (the negative effect of one is modified by the other).
feature_063 and feature_092 interact in their effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.021 per 1-SD x 1-SD; trending but not significant at alpha 0.05.
Interaction coefficient = -0.022, p=0.22. No detectable interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for clinical features (feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_035, feature_057), feature_005 (race) remains independently associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: LRT: full multivariable model with vs without C(feature_005), 4 df.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted LR p = 0.009. Adjusted ORs (vs reference 'asian'): black 0.97 (p=0.62); hispanic 1.05 (p=0.46); other 0.89 (p=0.26); white 1.08 (p=0.13). Joint test marginally significant; magnitudes small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for clinical features and race, feature_087 (insurance) remains associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: LRT: full model + C(feature_087) vs full model, 3 df
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted LR p = 0.47. Insurance class adds no detectable information about response after adjusting for clinical covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_093=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than those with feature_093=0.
Patients with feature_121=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than those with feature_121=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_121=1 (n=4,143): rate 15.64% vs feature_121=0 rate 17.02%. OR = 0.90.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014=1 is associated with a higher rate of objective_response than feature_014=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_014=1 (n=20,020): rate 17.35% vs feature_014=0 (n=29,980): rate 16.61%. OR = 1.05; small effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035=1 on objective_response is modified by race (feature_005); the relative effect differs across racial groups.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified ORs for feature_035=1: white OR=1.50 (p=2.6e-15), hispanic OR=1.31 (p=0.012), black OR=1.33 (p=0.025), asian OR=1.31 (p=0.13), other OR=1.47 (p=0.20). Interaction LR p = 0.73; benefit is qualitatively consistent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the ordinal feature feature_125 are associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher levels of feature_045, feature_096, feature_064, or feature_042 are associated with a different rate of objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_125', data=df).fit()  # treat as numeric trend
analyses.result_summary: feature_125 trend: log-OR per level = -0.023, OR = 0.977. Marginal effect.

analyses.code: Linear-trend logistic for each ordinal feature
analyses.result_summary: Trend p-values: feature_045 p=0.070, feature_096 p=0.51, feature_064 p=0.21, feature_042 p=0.35. None reach alpha 0.05; only feature_125 (above) shows a marginal monotonic trend.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035 on objective_response varies across feature_006 tertiles, with the effect smallest in the youngest tertile and largest in middle/older tertiles.

analyses.code: Stratified logistic of feature_035 within feature_006 tertiles
analyses.result_summary: Tertile 0 (youngest, ~20-52): OR=1.30 (p=2.5e-4). Tertile 1 (middle, ~52-69): OR=1.63 (p=7.7e-12). Tertile 2 (oldest, ~69-100): OR=1.42 (p=4.6e-6). Largest absolute and relative benefit in middle tertile, but interaction test (Iteration 9) was non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 80 binary features, only a small minority (<10%) are individually associated with objective_response at alpha=0.05; most features carry no detectable signal.
feature_035 x feature_087 (insurance) interaction: the relative benefit of feature_035 differs across insurance categories.

analyses.code: Count of binary features with univariate p<0.05.
analyses.result_summary: Of 80 binary features, only 5 reach p<0.05 univariately: feature_035 (p=4e-18), feature_093 (p=0.005), feature_121 (p=0.024), feature_014 (p=0.031), feature_012 (p=0.049). The signal is concentrated in a small number of variables; most binary features are uninformative for objective_response.
LR p = 0.091. Borderline only; no clear evidence of effect modification by insurance class.
